verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 is a tetrameric IP3-gated calcium release channel resident in the ER membrane. Its architecture is a 'mushroom-like' structure with the six-helix TM stalk inserted in the ER membrane and the entire ligand-binding cap (SD, IBC, regulatory, CTD domains) exposed to the cytosol — there is no extracellular-facing domain accessible from outside the plasma membrane. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet display in cancer, apoptosis, heat shock, or activation; ER-resident topology has no extracellular face at any cell state; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no PM surface pool documented in any lineage or developmental stage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool in surface proteomics or flow cytometry on intact cells; HPA reports cytoplasmic expression only; (5) stable_surface_attachment — not secreted, no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to plasma membrane. ITPR1-IgG autoantibodies (anti-Sj) target the intracellular protein in fixed/permeabilized tissue, not a surface-exposed epitope. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic exists. Verdict: endomembrane_resident.
